entry.title: Robust authentication for External candidates in SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Customer Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP SuccessFactors Community,

Let me introduce you to the robust authentication mechanism for External candidates in SAP SuccessFactors using SAP Customer Data Cloud, released in Q4 2024.

Business Context

In today’s fast-paced digital world, job applicants no longer want to take the time to create an account or fill out long forms when applying for a position. Instead, they prefer to apply quickly and effortlessly using their existing social login credentials or mobile number. This not only saves time but also streamlines the application process, making it more convenient for candidates.

However, while speed and convenience are essential, we cannot overlook the importance of security. As cyber threats continue to evolve, it’s crucial for employers and online platforms to ensure that their job application systems are secure. One effective way to enhance security is by implementing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). MFA adds an extra layer of protection by requiring users to verify their identity with more than just a password. Even if a password is compromised, the additional authentication steps—such as a one-time code sent to a mobile device or an authentication app—significantly reduce the risk of unauthorized access.

SAP SuccessFactors recognizes this need and has taken significant steps to enhance the login process for external candidates. By integrating fast and easy login options with multi-factor authentication (MFA), along with ensuring GDPR compliance, SAP SuccessFactors has provided a robust and secure authentication mechanism using SAP Customer Data Cloud.

What is SAP Customer Data Cloud?

The SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC) offers the opportunity to increase customer and brand loyalty and create more transparent, secure experiences across all channels at the same time.With SAP CDC, preferences, permissions, and access are managed and synchronized centrally – for all customers and across the entire SAP system environment . The SAP Customer Data Cloud ensures the security of customer data across all contact points that customers have with companies.

CDC console is at the heart of the product. All the Configurations, Settings, Identity Access, Customer Insights, Reports, Password policies etc can be configured using the console. Basically the implementation of Customer Data Cloud is done via the Console as shown below.

Unlocking the Benefits of SAP CDC with SAP SuccessFactors.

  1. Registration via phone number & social logins 

     

  2. No mandatory email registration

  3. Customizable login & registration workflows

    SAP Customer Data Cloud offers 24 social login providers, allowing customers to easily add the specific social logins they require for a seamless user experience.

     

  4. Enhanced security with One-Time Password (OTP)

  5. Support for Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

     

Licensing  Model

SAP Customer Data Cloud is free of charge and Customers need to purchase SMS configuration providers only for mobile verification.

Data center availability

SAP Customer Data Cloud is hosted in 4 Data centers Australia, China, Europe and the United States. BizX Datacenter will be mapped to the corresponding SAP Customer Data Cloud Datacenter.Refer to SAP Help Portal to check the mapping.

 

Pre-requisites to enable SAP Customer Data Cloud in SAP SuccessFactors

  • DPCS 2.0 enabled
  • "Use email as external applicant user ID" is enabled  in Provisioning
  • Mobile Apply enabled

How do we enable this feature?

Let’s understand the steps involved in Enabling SAP Customer Data Cloud in SAP SuccessFactors.

Once the pre-requisites are completed, the admin should be able to view the section Career Site with SAP Customer Data Cloud for External Candidates.

  1. Initiate Set up and Candidate data migration :

Once the customer initiates the setup and data migration SAP Customer Data Cloud instance is created and all the necessary settings are created/mapped to the CDC’s instance.  The external candidate details like FirstName, LastName, Email, password-related fields , consent details, phone number, country, etc will migrated to the CDC‘s data center.

  2. Manage Console Integration and External Candidate Authentication

This new RBP page is created to provide few configuration mapping and allows to invite users(typically admins) to provide access to CDC’s console.Using CDC’s console, admins can tailor the screens, add required social login identity providers and many more based on their requirements. Refer to the SAP Help Portal on how to add a required identity provider.

3. Enable Customer Data Cloud instance for External Candidate authentication

At this point, CDC’s instance is available , the external candidate is migrated successfully and  all the screens and settings are tailored as per customer’s requirement.You are now ready to activate  CDC for external candidate authentication.

Note : Candidate data excluding credentials is consistently synchronized between CDC and RCM .

For detailed information on setting up the instance, refer to the blog written by @PrashantKumarSF 

Refer to the video to view how enhanced authentication for external candidates.

 Conclusion

I hope you found the enhanced authentication and easy login features helpful and this blog post has provided valuable insights into its concepts. Whether you're just getting started or looking to configure it to suit your needs, I trust you now understand how it works.

If you have any questions or feedback, leave me a comment or ask them in the SAP community or the SAP SuccessFactors community.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to handle grandfathered cases in the a...
entry.content:
Hi @vivekanandanp 

There is no way in standard, you would need to build lots of custom fields in EC for this to work.

We've built it for customers before but the heavy work is in EC (Create new fields in Comp Info to store the Pay Range min, the Pay range mid and Pay Range max, then build the business rules to update a custom compa ratio field based on whether the person is in a grandfather situation or not).

All the best

Xavier

Refer to the video to view how enhanced authentication for external candidates.

I hope you found the enhanced authentication and easy login features helpful and this blog post has provided valuable insights into its concepts. Whether you're just getting started or looking to configure it to suit your needs, I trust you now understand how it works.

If you have any questions or feedback, leave me a comment or ask them in the SAP community or the SAP SuccessFactors community.

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @YannickRobin,

Thanks for sharing, I went thru the article and have 2 questions. It would be great that you could follow up please:

- Given example of "export MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=8" does it mean this node is having 4 cores? I am confused that in before those settings your environment has been configured with 10 cores, which supposes to be resulting to "export MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=20"? Can I confirm that "10 cores" and "export MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=8" are different environments?

- I understand that we are about to intervene MALLOC_ARENA_MAX by setting up 2 catalina versions. Decoding base 64 value from ccv2.file.override.71.content I could see the the content is exactly identical with our catalina.sh as script below (scripted on hac)

import org.apache.commons.io.FileUtils;
String contents = FileUtils.readFileToString(new File("/opt/tomcat/bin/catalina.sh"), "UTF-8")

Now my concern is, if we force ccv2 to maintain override version of /opt/tomcat/bin/catalina-original.sh does that mean we are giving up the benefit of new catalina.sh would be aligned automatically once SAP release new update? Do we have any other way that allows to set that variable and not to touch on original version?

 

Thanks,

Duc.

entry.title: How to perform a reversal for a stored material in SAP Public Cloud?
entry.content:
In the SAP Public Cloud system, a goods receipt for serialized materials was posted to the WM warehouse upon receiving the delivery. However, since the serialized materials were previously stored under a different warehouse number in WM stock, the ERP goods receipt could not be completed despite the WM goods receipt being finalized. As a result, there is a stock inconsistency between WM and ERP.

How can a reversal process be performed for a material that has completed the WM goods receipt and has been stored at its final destination?

entry.title: Re: IDOC is send from ECC with status 03 but not showing...
entry.content:
Hi Mahesh

about the first picture this does not need to be an issue - it's pointing to those Destinations in NWA that are relevent that Metadata are read:

So if the values of XI_IDOC_DEFAULT_DESTINATION points to the right backend - than it's fine. If you are integrating more than one backend - you have to setup accordingly different destinations and configure it into the inboundRA.

But if this would be an error - the messages would be stuck on error in SM58 - like in this wiki:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/techtsg/3362710211.html

If they are don't visible in ABAP backend anymore they are in PI. 

You might check IDOC adapter monitor https://server:port/PIMON

If you don't find something either - maybe the monitoring/logging parameter are set to zero and you don't see anything? But i guess at least in the channel monitor you should see something like:

Hope that helps.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Create ABAP program to send SQL command to another HA...
entry.content:
Hi Remi ! thank you so much for your reply I really appreciate this !

The request was to create a program to execute SQL commands to create or delete hana users not to execute sql commands in tables. Did you already do something similar ? The idea was to excute DBCO connection to a specific system when executing the abap program, then execute sql commande to insert or delete hana user then close DBCO connection. 

I never did something like this, this is why I posted this request.

I received the information that we cannot do this kind of action in mass so execute this kind of sql action in many systems one after one. If we want to do something like this with a console, we need to connect into each system then execute sql command and could be a waist of time.

Thank you so much for your help !

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2024年04月 – 2024年06月）
entry.content:
本文主要收集了与SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition集成扩展相关的常见问题(2024年04月 – 2024年06月)，按照主要问询对象API，CDS View，CPI，自定义字段和逻辑以及集成迁移相关对常见问题进行了大致的分类。客户可以点击下方链接查看到相关问题。

 

接口与集成相关问题

 

问题：客户想知道采购订单审批相关的API或CDS

解答：目前没有准确的相关的API或CDS，可以参考下面的CDS：

I_WorkflowStatusOverview

I_WorkflowStatusDetails

Scenario ID：WS00800238           Workflow Name：Workflow for Purchase Order

 

问题：采购申请API上如何添加组件的Subcontracting item，因委外采购申请需从外围系统发起，需做系统对接

解答：通过标准采购申请API无法添加组件

 

问题：客户想要知道是否可以通过API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV来更改supplier batch字段？

解答：当使用API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV做posting GR的时候，不支持输入supplier batch字段，因此不能通过API更新。

 

问题：用户需要S/4HANA Cloud消息推送与企业微信的集成

解答：可参考以下白皮书文档：

https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/qxvTx2x?ltr=a&rc=10&doi=SAP1078633

 

问题：采购订单备注字段的API接口如何获取

解答：通过以下接口可以获取采购订单明细的备注字段：

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/API_PURCHASEORDER_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrderItem/4500169906/0010/_PurchaseOrderItemNote

 

问题：客户在使用journal entry的SOAP API创建的时候出错，是关于自定义的字段出错。

解答：关于如何在journal entry日记账分录API中创建和使用自定义字段，可以参考以下博客文章：

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/guidelines-for-creating-and-enabling-extension-field-for-journal-entry-api/ba-p/13421678

 

问题：系统登录和使用非常慢，转圈，平均打开每个页面需要2分钟，外围系统接口交互异常，处理起来也频繁出错

解答：是由于数据库服务器上的memory issue，数据库自动恢复了，数据库和应用层的连接之后也恢复了。经过调查研究，memory issue是由于HANA DB的一个bug造成的，新版本的HANA DB已经修复此bug。

 

问题：使用接口 /sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetcreatemain 或/sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetchange 创建或修改固定资产成功或失败都没有信息返回。

解答：因为是异步API，所以是正常行为。如果需要查看信息，可以去Message Dashboard查看。

 

问题：客户想要知道是否有接口可以新增工单组件？

解答：如果是三系统客户，可以使用以下接口做扩展开发：

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_PRODUCTIONORDERTP

但是，如果是二系统客户，确实没有这样的一个可用接口。

 

问题：客户想要通过API接口提取SAP内的所有会计凭证，例如包括费用报销、销售成本、生产成本、付款、收款等业务场景，想咨询应该使用API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM还是API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV？这两个有什么区别？

解答：API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM可以读取General ledger view的journal entry行项目；API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV可以读取entry view的journal entry，但是它不是用来抽取大量数据的。具体可参考以下两个链接：

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/80214b5ede2f4e97b640137a4eca208a.html?locale=en-US&q=API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/c024170fa7af40878975e218f3426387.html?locale=en-US&q=API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV

 

问题：客户通过自定义CDS的接口读取数据，发现返回数据特别慢。

解答：发现在系统慢的时候，同时有大量的并行的API调用API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV/A_BillOfMaterial，这些并行处理占据了大部分的会话进程，导致其他的调用非常慢。为了避免这个问题，推荐把这些调用绑定，而不是并行大量地同时调用。

 

问题：通过接口  /API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV/A_PurgInfoRecdOrgPlantData (PurchasingInfoRecord='xxx',PurchasingInfoRecordCategory='0',PurchasingOrganization='xxx',Plant='xxx') 来修改采购信息记录，payload如下：

{

    "IsMarkedForDeletion": false

}

但是报错：

error": {

        "code": "/IWBEP/CM_MGW_RT/022",

        "message": {

            "lang": "zh",

            "value": "Please check key fields of URI and payload"

        },

        "innererror": {

            "application": {

                "component_id": "MM-PUR-VM-REC",

                "service_namespace": "/SAP/",

                "service_id": "API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV",

                "service_version": "0001"

            },

            "transactionid": "335BF710CCFC0210E006645265FF29DA",

            "timestamp": "20240516135151.5639670",

            "Error_Resolution": {

                "SAP_Transaction": "For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details",

                "SAP_Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"

            },

解答：在payload中，除了要更改的字段，把key fields也放到payload中，修改后的payload如下：

{

    "PurchasingInfoRecord": "xxx",

    "PurchasingInfoRecordCategory": "0",

    "PurchasingOrganization":"xxx",

    "Plant":"xxx",

    "IsMarkedForDeletion": false

}

 

 

 

CDS视图相关问题

 

问题：税码对应的税率在哪个CDS或者API接口可以取到？

解答：在目前的版本中，没有可用的CDS可以取到税码对应的税率，用户需要使用SSCUI 101016来查看税码和税率。目前有以下类似的CIP请求，有类似需求可以打开链接投票：

CDS view for BSET table

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/231702

Release Used in Cloud Development for I_TaxCode

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/317901

Extension of the already existing CDS view I_TaxCode with the actual TaxRate

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/311388

 

问题：在自定义CDS视图中不能新建CDS视图

解答：首先第一，创建自定义CDS视图不能在P系统中做，必须在Q/D系统中新建之后再传输。

然后如果在Q/D系统中也不能新建，则需要去检查权限问题，是否有需要的角色和业务目录，在restriction里面的写入权限是不是un-restricted。

 

 

扩展开发相关问题

 

问题：对于科目类型为M/A/S的日记账分录行项目，自定义字段可以被更改。客户想要更改科目类型为D的行项目中的自定义字段，想问需要在业务上下文FINS_CODING_BLOCK或FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_ITEM中创建自定义字段吗？

解答：按照Note https://me.sap.com/notes/3448139 ， 增加的自定义字段只能针对科目类型为M/A/S的行项目更改或保存，不支持科目类型为D或者K的。如需实现功能，按照KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2963059  提交新功能。

 

问题：客户通过自定义业务对象自开发的App，在生产系统中找不到了，但是在测试系统中还是可以看到。

解答：这个通过自定义业务对象自开发的App，分配了业务目录SAP_CORE_BC_EXT，但是该业务目录已经弃用。需要把新的业务目录分配给自定义业务对象就可以解决该问题。

 

 

其他问题

 

问题：迁移主控室创建物料主数据提示错误“Virus scanner could not * *”

解答：virus scanner configuration里有问题，联系SAP operation团队帮助修复问题。

 

问题：导入产品主数据，发生错误"The custom property LANGUAGE is missing in the uploaded file."

解答：导致这个问题的原因是模板文件格式的问题，复制数据到模板里的时候，只拷贝值，不用复制格式等其他信息。

 

问题：客户的IAS打不开了：https://XXX.accounts.ondemand.com/admin/

解答：使用新连接：

https://XXX.accounts.sapcloud.cn/admin

 

问题：使用App Mass Changes to Purchasing Info Records来修改物料的采购信息记录的价格（PPR0）时，根据模板导入，但是系统只更新了第一条记录的价格，其他的记录没有更新成功。

解答：如何通过导入Excel文件的方式来修改采购信息记录的价格，可参考以下链接：

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/offline-price-update-for-purchasing-info-record-via-microsoft-excel/ba-p/13538542

 

问题：是否有方法可以进行批次的批量转移？（Mass transfer stock from batch to batch）

解答：目前没有app或transaction可以做mass goods movement posting。在MIGO/Post Goods Movement里，选择Detail Data section，然后可以复制/粘贴物料、plant等信息。但是不能通过导入Excel文件的方式来实现批量操作。

 

 

 

 

以上为本次分享的全部内容。若想要了解往期更多有关集成扩展的常问问题，可以点击下方链接进行查看：

 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年01月 – 2022年03月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年04月 – 2022年06月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年07月 – 2022年09月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年10月 – 2022年12月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年01月 – 2023年03月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年04月 – 2023年06月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年07月 – 2023年09月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年10月 – 2023年12月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2024年01月 – 2024年03月)：click here.

entry.title: Re: error in cl_sxml_string_writer, sXML Library
entry.content:
Hi Monika,

Development released a solution with SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2502. There are now header options for the sXML Library. These are the options:

  • CO_OPT_VAL_NO (no XML header; default)
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version)
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version and encoding)

You can find more in the documentation: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENABAP_SXML_LIB_RENDER.html (hint section).

Best regards,

Lena

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 5: SAP BTP Cost Management
entry.content:
This episode discusses managing costs for the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This can be done at the global account level within SAP BTP. Users can track costs by service or account and even export data for further analysis. For more detailed monitoring, there is a community-driven application hosted on GitHub that offers enhanced features for tracking consumption, setting rules, and receiving alerts. This is application that runs on top of SAP BTP and provides additional functionality beyond the default SAP BTP cost monitoring tools.

Takeaways

  • Monitoring costs is crucial when using cloud applications.
  • Costs can be tracked at the global account level.
  • Authorization at the global account level is required to view cost details.
  • You can export cost details to Excel for further analysis.
  • The platform provides functionality to monitor costs by service or subaccount.
  • A community-driven consumption monitoring app is available on GitHub.
  • The GitHub app is created by an individual from SAP, but it is not an official SAP product.
    • GitHub - SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor: Monitors the CPEA credit consumption of your global account and alerts on increased usage
  • The app offers additional functionality for setting cost rules and monitoring alerts.
  • The app helps users gain better visibility and control over their platform costs.

entry.title: Re: Where are images stored when uploaded directly in SAP...
entry.content:
SAC stores images different than BO. You have 2 (and a half) option:

1. static image - will be stored inside the story definition on SAC (no sharing between stories)

2. dynamic image - consumed from a model, see answer from @sathyapachipala 

3. custom widget - implement a simple custom widget to show pictures e.g. from a remote URL (in this case just the image URL will be stored in the story definition on SAC)

General documentation is here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/9e8f71edceb94bc0ab5ccb774e5e81dd.html

However, I don't understand the requirement from the client. The story definition itself is anyhow stored on SAC and may even contain metadata which is more sensitive than an image. What is the reasoning behind this?

entry.title: Re: Implementing CRUD Operations in OData v4
entry.content:
We have referred the documentation for "CRUD" and implemented Read and Create operations, but "UPDATE" is not working as desired. There is no "patch/update" call is being called in the network tab instead of showing error. 

Here is the sample code:

    // Bind the context to the entity

                var oContext = oMainModel.bindContext(sPath);

 oContext.requestObject().then(function(oEntity) {

                    console.log("Entity before update:", oEntity);

                    // Apply updates to the entity properties

                    Object.assign(oEntity, updateData);

                    console.log("Entity after update:", oEntity);

       

                    // Check if there are pending changes before submitting

                    console.log("Pending changes in model:", oMainModel.hasPendingChanges(oMainModel.getUpdateGroupId() || "$auto"));

       

                    // Submit the changes using a batch request

                    oMainModel.submitBatch(oMainModel.getUpdateGroupId() || "$auto")

                        .then((oResponse) => {

                            console.log("PATCH request successful, response:", oResponse);

                            resolve();

                        })

                        .catch((oError) => {

                            console.error("PATCH request failed:", oError);

                            if (oError.response) {

                                console.error("Response status:", oError.response.status);

                                console.error("Response text:", oError.response.responseText);

                            }

                            reject(oError);

                        });

Is this the correct approach? if not please give suggestions...

entry.title: Operational Reporting on SAP S/4HANA live data
entry.content:
Summary

This article describes architecture options for SAP S/4HANA operational reporting with live data needs. Analytical applications should access data directly from the application tables in SAP S/4HANA, avoiding data replication to another application due to latency concerns. 

Possible options for this scenario are the following:

  • SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs
  • SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA analytical CDS Views (Queries) for flexible self-service reporting via SAP Query Browser

Business Requirements

Business users need an accurate, real-time picture of the current business situation based on KPIs. Real-time analytics and reporting capabilities in SAP S/4HANA are needed to fulfil the business requirements.

Requirements may be linked to business process steps or should to be provided as separate applications (e.g., outstanding payments of a specific customer for the last 12 months) with flexible slice-and-dice capabilities.

Operational reports/analytical applications should be accessible based on S/4HANA user roles integrated into SAP Fiori Launchpad.

Solution Options

Most commonly used options for the operational reporting scenario on live data are the following:

  • SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs
  • SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA CDS Views (Queries) for flexible self-service reporting via SAP Query Browser

These solution options are applicable for SAP S/4HANA public Cloud, SAP S/4HANA private Cloud or SAP S/4HANA on premise deployments.

Characterization of the Solution Options
 
 SAP S/4HANA embedded Analytics with analytical SAP Fiori UIs

SAP S/4HANA (on-premise, private or public Cloud) provides out of the box reporting functionality via SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics. It is a fully integrated analytics solution based on SAP Fiori and is included in standard SAP S/4HANA license or subscription fee.

How to use SAP Embedded Analytics?
SAP S/4HANA embedded analytic applications are based on SAP Fiori (Elements) technology and can be accessed via SAP Fiori Launchpad.
SAP provides several types of analytic apps, such as:

  • Overview pages
  • Analytic List Pages
  • KPI Tiles
  • Multidimensional Reporting Client+
  • Review Booklets
  • Multidimensional Analysis

More than 500 predefined Analytical SAP Fiori Apps are provided and can be accessed in SAP Fiori Launchpad as long as they have been generally activated and you are assigned to the role, which includes the app.

Additional information about SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics can be found here:

  • SAP Help
  • SAP Learning: Going Deeper with SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics 
    (free of charge 10h virtual learning class)

ABAP Core data services (CDS) is the backbone of this solution and provide access to the data in the raw data tables in SAP S/4HANA. No additional persistency is required and CDS views are purely virtual structures.
SAP provides a wide range of predefined analytical Fiori Apps supporting over 20 industries and 50 different lines of business. A full list can be found via SAP Fiori Application Reference Library


After customer has chosen the analytical Fiori applications and activated them in the S/4HANA System, 
business users are able to personalize these Fiori applications by using variants or creating own tiles.
Power user are even more flexible and able to adjust and extend most of the SAP Fiori applications in a graphical editor.  
Details see: Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation.

It is also possible to create own SAP Fiori application for analytic purpose based on predefined floor plans, such as overview page or analytical list page.
Developing Apps with SAP Fiori Elements -> Developing Apps with SAP Fiori Elements

 SAP Analytics Cloud with live data connection to SAP S/4HANA

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is recommended for advanced reporting applications, dashboards or planning applications beyond the capabilities provided by SAP Fiori.
As the strategic Reporting & Planning solution from SAP, SAC makes it easy to visualize data based on your own needs and corporate design guidelines.

Besides the “data import connection” SAC provides the so-called “live connection” for other SAP solutions. 'Live connections' are widely used by SAP customers and do not replicate application data into the cloud and keep the data transfer within your coprporate network.
SAC mainly serves as design time for the reporting application. At runtime only meta data is provided by SAC. The data itself for the report is directly fetched from the SAP source system (e.g. SAP S/4HANA) and sent directly to the browser of the business user without leaving the corporate network.
This is a unique differentiator compared to other non-SAP reporting solutions. With this approach business users connect with their SAP S/4HANA backend user to the S/4HANA system and utilizing the existing authorization concept.
For details, please check out SAP Analytics Cloud Connectivity Guidelines.

How to use SAP Analytics Cloud with live connectivity from SAP S/4HANA side for operational reporting:

  • Execute SAP S/4HANA Fiori App ‘Manage KPIs and Reports’
  • Go to ‘Stories’
  • Create Story (SAC needs to be configured before in SAP S/4HANA according to description Manage KPIs and Reports - Stories )

SAC integrates with SAP S/4HANA via analytical CDS views and InA protocol (similar like a BW query) or via OData. The SAC story is served with data directly from the CDS view at runtime.

Modern Dashboard design via SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

SAP also provides predefined SAC Dashboards based on SAP S/4HANA live data. Those scenarios are listed in the Analytics Content Network:

Source: Extended Content Inventory

Customers can also define their own SAC stories based on SAP S/4HANA (analytical) CDS views. It is even possible to blend data between two SAP S/4HANA systems (SAP Analytics Cloud Help - Blend Data ).

SAC stories can be integrated seamlessly from UI perspective into the SAP Fiori Launchpad framework. Shared UI paradigms such as intent-based navigation optimize the experience for SAP S/4HANA business users. It is e.g. possible to jump from a sales order number within the SAC story directly into the SAP Fiori app for maintaining this specific sales order (SAP Fiori app: Manage Sales Order).
Details see: SAP S/4HANA on premise: Integration of SAP Analytics Cloud optimizes business user experience.

Remark: This intend-based navigation and the support of variants are very strong features and key differentiators for SAC. 

 Analytical CDS Views (queries) for flexible self-service reporting via
 SAP Query Browser

The third option for operational reporting which is introduced in this decision paper is related to flexible querying on S/4HANA live data.
Customers also call this option Self-Service Querying, similar like in the past with SAP Query with Infosets in SAP ECC.

How to use SAP Query Browser:

  • Execute the Fiori app ‘Query Browser’
  • Choose the specific analytic CDS View / query which you are interested in
  • Execute and 'Open for Analysis'
  • After entering the multidimensional Reporting Client querying can be done in pivot-table like style.

This option provided a simple way for table-based adhoc reporting with slice and dice functionality and limited graphical reporting features.

 

For further information about embedded analytics in general, including usage of reporting apps, Query Browser and SAC please take a look to the SAP Learning: Going Deeper with SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics

Decision Tree

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics (Standard Fiori apps or generic Query Browser) and SAC provide an excellent base for operational reporting on SAP S/4HANA live data.

All these three options can be seamlessly integrated into the SAP Fiori Launchpad, utilize existing user setups/authorizations in SAP S/4HANA, and do not require additional persistency or data flow logic. Development, operations, and maintenance costs are low, with minimal development effort compared to other solutions.

Context information from SAP S/4HANA, such as hierarchy/currency/unit/time conversions, is reused without additional effort.

Review Booklets and a new Multidimensional Analytics (MDA) client are under development, further enriching SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics capabilities (see SAP S/4HANA product roadmap).

entry.title: Re: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content:
Datasphere allows us to merge the outputs of 12 nodes using a single union operation. This streamlined approach eliminates the need for multiple unions, simplifying the view while ensuring that all node outputs are consolidated into one cohesive view.

Thanks,

AS

entry.title: How to read and parse SAMLResponse in custom Odata read call
entry.content:
Hello All,

Can you please suggest if SAML Response can be read in a custom Odata Read call in below scenario.

1. When user logs in to the portal, SSO authentication is done. During this authentication process, the logged in user is changed based on the configuration in USREXTID table. For example, if user A is maintained as substitute of user B, then user A will access all applications as user B. This is as per SAML configurations.

2. After authentication, while loading the landing page in Fiori there is a Odata call to backend to fetch user attributes. In this call, we are able to fetch attributes of only UserB (as per above configuration). But in this call, we also want to read the actual user ID who is accessing the application. Is it possible to read SAML response or NameID attribute in odata read call.

3. We do not want to read the data from table USREXTID, because same user can have multiple substitute users. Hence, we want to know if its possible to read and parse SAMLresponse.

Best Regards!

entry.title: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Innovations in FPS3
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

Welcome back! In this edition we bring you the latest innovations and features in localization of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

Here we are talking about a comprehensive solution that handles subscription and usage-based models. It provides a standardized approach to recurring billing, invoicing, tax compliance, and automation.

With each release, SAP introduces localized country/region versions and new functions to assist customers with tax compliance, billing, invoicing, automation, and streamlined processes. This not only helps reduce compliance risks and costs but also improves the sustainability of tax operations.

For example, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud FPS3 update, several countries and regions were incorporated into the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization.

For more information, I invite you to visit the following pages: 

  • Road Map Explorer: Future innovations planned 
  • SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization on SAP Help Portal:  https://help.sap.com
  • What’s New: What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Blog Post: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Sustainable Compliance for Your Recurring Revenue Business

The purpose of this post is to show you how SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Localization is a game-changer for businesses looking to streamline their financial operations while ensuring compliance with local regulations. Stay tuned for the next update!

I hope you found this blog post interesting. If so, I invite you to follow my profile and the tags SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. Also, feel free to leave a comment or a suggestion in the comment section.

Thank you for choosing SAP to run your business complaint with local tax authorities!

Hi @matheusog 

sorry for the delay, I hope you have found a solution in the meanwhile.
The launchpad does not support multiple apps in the subaccount, which means that a shared reuse app-host must not contain a manifest.json with sap.app/type: application. Could it be that "br.agr.fs.ct.motor.lib" is declared as application?
With the new CDM based approach you will no longer run into this limitation, as every business solution is completely separated from others.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Problems with CAP App cds.persistence.skip
entry.content:
An entity annotated with `@cds.persistence.skip` or `@cds.persistence.exists` will not result in a database table being created for you. The `@cds.persistence.exists` annotation is mostly for scenarios, where you have an existing persistence which is outside of you CAP model. E.g. native HANA artifacts… Please read our documentation for more on that.

That being said, if you expose an entity with this annotation, you have to implement your own logic to e.g. READ data from this entity, which is not part of your CAP model.

entry.title: Re: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content: If you want to only send deltas to the S3 bucket then you will need a transformation flow that sends only deltas to a new local table from the existing delta enabled table then another replication flow to send the deltas from the new table to AWS

entry.title: Help me with this code to display the FG code against each material code.
entry.content:
This is my abap code block

DATA: it_fg_code TYPE TABLE OF mast-matnr,   " Table to store FG Codes
      matnr1      TYPE mast-matnr.           " Variable for single FG Code

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE text-001.

SELECT-OPTIONS material FOR mast-matnr.
SELECT-OPTIONS plant FOR mast-werks.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK B1.

START-OF-SELECTION.

" Step 1: Fetch FG Code from MAST table for material type 'FERT'
SELECT DISTINCT mast~matnr
  INTO TABLE IT_MTYPE1
  FROM mast
  INNER JOIN mara ON mast~matnr = mara~matnr
  WHERE mast~matnr IN material
    AND mara~MTART = 'FERT'.

" Step 2: Fetch details from MAST, STPO, and MAKt for materials and their exploded parts
SELECT mast~werks
       mast~stlnr
       mast~matnr
       makt~maktx
       idnrk
       stpo~menge
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE itm_details
  FROM mast AS mast
  INNER JOIN stpo AS stpo ON mast~stlnr = stpo~stlnr
  INNER JOIN makt AS makt ON mast~matnr = makt~matnr
  WHERE mast~matnr IN material
    AND werks IN plant
    AND stpo~stlty = 'M'
    AND mast~stlal = mast~stlal.

IF NOT itm_details[] IS INITIAL.

  " Step 3: Fetch location data
  SELECT matnr
         werks
         lgpro
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE it_loc
    FROM marc
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN itm_details
    WHERE werks = itm_details-werks
    AND matnr = itm_details-matnr.

ENDIF.

IF NOT itm_details[] IS INITIAL.

  " Step 4: Fetch verification data for the materials
  SELECT mast~matnr
         mast~werks
         verid
         bdatu
         adatu
         mkal~stlal
         bstma
         idnrk
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE it_ver
    FROM mast AS mast
    INNER JOIN mkal AS mkal ON mast~matnr = mkal~matnr
    INNER JOIN stpo AS stpo ON mast~stlnr = stpo~stlnr
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN itm_details
    WHERE mast~werks = itm_details-werks
    AND mast~matnr = itm_details-matnr.

ENDIF.

* Step 5: Loop through itm_details and update FG Code for 'FERT' type materials
LOOP AT itm_details INTO DATA(ls_item).

  " Check if material type is 'FERT' by checking against IT_MTYPE1
  READ TABLE it_mtype1 INTO matnr1 WITH KEY matnr = ls_item-matnr.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    " Assign FG Code to the structure only for 'FERT' materials
    ls_item-fg_code = matnr1.  " Assign FG Code to the structure
  ELSE.
    " For exploded parts, set FG code to blank or empty
    ls_item-fg_code = ''.  " No FG code for exploded parts
  ENDIF.

The above mentioned code is displaying this output

I want on the FERT codes to be displayed against it.

entry.title: Re: Monitoring Integration Issues with the New “Issues Detected” Event in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear @rashmi_vanaja,

Good blog, and very detailed.  Thanks for taking the time to document it.

However, I am trying to understand the differences between:
* Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)
* Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Grouped)
* Issues Detected in XXXX Messages

(I am using XXXX because I'm working with IDocs)

Anyway, I did a lot of tests, and I cannot see any differences between the Metrics. I can change them, and they will behave the same way.

According to my test, those Metrics bring default parameters that give you the possibility to get a specific event, but If we change the parameters, the result will be the same.

For example, if I select"Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Single), and I remove in "Grouping" tab, only the Message ID (that is unique), I have the same behavior as "Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Grouped)" because there is no Message ID.

The Frequency of event calculation in the metric "Issues Detected in XXXX Messages" metric is the same as the Single or Grouped (depending on the parameters) because the job is the same that was set in /sdf/alm_setup (in our case every 5 minutes)

I would like to confirm this from your side, if you notice the same, because those metrics are listed as they are different, but are only like templates to start to configure your metrics.

Conclusion 1: I can use the "Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)" metric, and change all the parameters inside to get the same result as "Erroneous XXXX Message Detected (Grouped)".
Conclusion 2: I can use the "Erroneous XXXX Messages Detected (Single)" metric, and change the status group parameters inside to get the same result as "Issues Detected in XXXX Messages".

I would like to have a confirmation of this because I cannot see any different behavior in them.

Question: Why there are 3 different metrics? in the end, we can have the same result.

Thanks!

entry.title: New Use Cases for SAP Connectivity in SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery
entry.content:
Developers today often face complex network topologies. You may be using a cloud service to run your continuous integration jobs, but important assets like source code or binary repositories could still be within your corporate network.

The SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service was designed with this challenge in mind. From the beginning, we integrated the SAP Connectivity service to allow cloning of source code from on-premises repositories. Later, we added more features:

  • Connecting to on-premises Sonar code scanners
  • Deploying on ABAP systems 

Now, we have taken it one step further, enabling you to use the connectivity feature in your own scripts and configurations. As an example, I will describe how to use an on-premises npm registry.

But before we dive into that, I would like to explain how Connectivity is integrated into the Continuous Integration and Delivery service.

Connecting It All

When your continuous integration (CI) build interacts with the outside world, whether to fetch a source file, download a dependency, post a report, or perform other actions, this interaction generally involves sending an HTTP request.

If your request needs to reach an on-premises system, certain requirements must be met for Connectivity to work properly. Assuming that your target system is configured in your cloud connector, the request must:

  • Be sent via a specific proxy.
  • Contain custom HTTP headers, allowing Connectivity to authenticate and route the request.

However, not every tool allows for the setting of arbitrary HTTP headers. Therefore, we decided to use a proxy whose task is to accept HTTP requests, add the required headers, and forward the requests to the Connectivity service.

If your job is configured to use Connectivity (via a credential representing a Cloud Connector), this proxy will run in a container within the run environment of your build and wait for incoming requests. Note that this environment is not accessible from the outside world, ensuring that only your containers can send requests to the proxy.

Here is a diagram visualizing this setup:

Example: Using an On-Premises npm Registry

Enough theory; let's work through a real use case. Consider the following scenario:

We want to build a JavaScript application using npm. The problem is that the application has dependencies defined in its `package.json` which are located in an npm registry hosted by your company, and this registry is not accessible from the internet.

We'll assume for this scenario that all the dependencies can be fetched from that corporate registry. It is a common practice to have corporate registries that mirror registry.npm.org. This setup allows you to maintain copies of your open-source dependencies which you can then scan. Additionally, your build process no longer depends on the availability of registry.npm.org.

Let's go through the setup step by step.

Prerequisites

  • You have a Cloud Connector running in your corporate network.
  • You have a subscription of the SAP Continuous Integration and Deliver Service.

Prepare the Cloud Connector

  1. Add the account where the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service is subscribed to your Cloud Connector.
    This is described in the Cloud Connector Documentation.
  2. In the Cloud Connector, under "Cloud To On-Premise", add your private registry.
    The configuration should look something like this:

Configure the Continuous Integration and Delivery Service

In the UI of the service:

  1. Create a Cloud Connector Credential:

    First, create a Cloud Connector credential containing the location ID of your cloud connector. Name this credential connector.

  2. Create a Job for Building/Testing/Deploying Your Project:

    Next, create a job to build, test, and deploy your project. We want to use the on-premises registry during the build stage. To achieve this, add the connector credentials as an additional credential to that stage.

    This configuration will ensure that during the build execution, an environment variable CONN_PROXY will be available, holding the URL of the proxy.

    Example Build Stage:

  3. Configure Additional Variables:

    In addition, configure the following additional variables:

    • NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL: info: This makes npm print more detailed information about what it is doing.
    • NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG: .cicd_npmrc: This makes npm use an npmrc file named .cicd_npmrc located in the project root.

    The last setting is particularly useful because it avoids altering the local build process of the project.

Prepare the Project

The only thing you need to do in the project is to add the npmrc file to be used in the central build. As mentioned above, the name should be .cicd_npmrc. Here is my file:

registry=http://on-prem-registry/
proxy=${CONN_PROXY}
replace-registry-host=always
 

Let's got through this line by line.

  • registry=http://on-prem-registry/: Here, I define the registry to be used. I refer to the registry by the virtual host name configured in the Cloud Connector. A port is not required because I used port 80, which is the standard port for the HTTP protocol. Note that I use HTTP and not HTTPS. This is due to a limitation of our current connectivity setup. However, it is not as bad as it sounds. Once the data reaches the proxy, it will be transmitted via an encrypted tunnel to the Cloud Connector.

  • proxy=${CONN_PROXY}: This defines that the proxy should be used. The actual URL is injected by the service. In practice, it will look like http://10.0.0.3:5003, where the port is mapped to the location ID of the Cloud Connector.

  • replace-registry-host=always: This is needed to avoid trouble with the package-lock.json files (in the project as well as in the dependencies). The package-lock.json is generated locally and contains registry URLs pointing to npm-registry.sap-dev.corp, the actual hostname of our internal registry. For the central build, another hostname (on-prem-registry) will be used, therefore the hostname has to be replaced.

That is all. Here is an excerpt from the build stage logs:

[2025-02-14T16:03:32.499Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Discovered package.json file package.json
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.499Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - running command: npm config get registry -ws=false -iwr
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.500Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using npm@10.8.2
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using node@v20.17.0
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info ok
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Discovered pre-configured npm registry registry with value http://on-prem-registry/
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z]
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - Running Install
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.501Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - running command: npm ci
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.762Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using npm@10.8.2
[2025-02-14T16:03:32.762Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm info using node@v20.17.0
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.688Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/xsenv/-/xsenv-4.2.0.tgz 725ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.688Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/e2e-trace/-/e2e-trace-4.1.0.tgz 766ms (cache miss)
[2025-02-14T16:03:33.948Z] info  npmExecuteScripts - npm http fetch GET 200 http://on-prem-registry/@sap/logging/-/logging-7.1.0.tgz 910ms (cache miss)

As intended, the dependencies are being pulled from our internal registry.

Outlook and Next Steps

At this point, you should have realized that Connectivity can now be used in each stage and even in your custom scripting through the Additional Commands feature.

From Continuous Integration and Delivery side, the next Connectivity related feature we will take on is supporting the SOCKS protocol. This will eliminate the current restriction against using HTTPS and enable advanced scenarios such as combining mTLS with Connectivity.
